title: Sweet Chili Glazed Chicken with Jasmine Rice & Snap Peas
description: EveryPlate weeknight staple — juicy chicken thighs glazed in a sticky sweet chili sauce, served over jasmine rice with crisp snap peas and a drizzle of sesame oil. Big flavors, minimal effort.
instructions: Cook rice according to package directions.
Pat chicken thighs dry and season with salt and pepper.
Mix sweet chili sauce, soy sauce, minced garlic, and ginger in a small bowl.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook 5–6 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (165°F).
Pour the sweet chili sauce mixture over the chicken. Cook 1–2 minutes, turning to coat, until the sauce thickens and glazes the chicken.
In the same pan (or a separate one), stir-fry snap peas with a splash of water and a pinch of salt for 2–3 minutes until bright green and crisp-tender.
Serve chicken and snap peas over rice. Drizzle with sesame oil and garnish with sliced scallions and sesame seeds.
tips: Sweet chili sauce is available in the Asian foods aisle of most grocery stores — Mae Ploy is a popular brand.
Chicken thighs are more forgiving than breasts and stay juicy even if slightly overcooked.
For extra caramelization, broil the glazed chicken for 2 minutes at the end.
This sauce also works great as a dipping sauce for spring rolls or dumplings.
